packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time
explicit_request: Reduce human intervention burden by keeping ThirdPerson regression proof on the human default lane, preserving hard stop/go and ownership boundaries, answering explicit questions directly, and using valid runtime evidence and root-cause discipline.

needs.label: Trustworthy default-lane proof
needs.description: Regression closure must name a repo-canonical human lane and rely on that lane's runtime proof rather than adjacent support-lane artifacts.

needs.label: Hard stop/go and ownership continuity
needs.description: STOP, PAUSE, continue-under-existing-ownership, and no-homework-back instructions must behave like hard execution state rather than soft tone cues.

needs.label: Direct answers in the requested shape
needs.description: Agree/disagree, yes/no, and simple factual questions need a direct line-one answer before continued work narration.

needs.label: Approval-ready review surfaces
needs.description: Plan and pass approval asks must include diffs, links, and scope so the human can approve without reconstructing state.

needs.label: Valid runtime evidence surfaces
needs.description: Claims about runtime defects must stay grounded in admissible in-lane evidence and narrow or withdraw when that evidence is invalidated.

needs.label: First-disagreement root-cause tracing
needs.description: Debugging must name the first concrete bad state and trace writers upstream instead of stalling at symptom-level tweaking.

suffering_signals.notes: OBSERVED: the human explicitly says time was wasted and course correction was required.
OBSERVED: profanity and sharp escalation indicate acute friction without implying diagnosis.
OBSERVED: the human frames judgment and supervision failures as unacceptable and recurring.

highest_leverage_context_to_fetch.context: Repo-local lane and proof contract from ThirdPerson/REGRESSION.md plus Manual PIE expectations from ThirdPerson/TESTING.md
highest_leverage_context_to_fetch.why: The packet repeatedly turns on what counts as valid default-lane proof and what does not.

highest_leverage_context_to_fetch.context: Exact stop/go and ownership language already present in the packet
highest_leverage_context_to_fetch.why: The repeated continuity burden is explicit enough that extra context fetch is lower value than enforcing the rule.

clarifying_question.reason: The packet already contains repeated explicit constraints; asking more during this rerun would add burden without resolving a blocker.

risk_notes: The implementation-thread transcript named in SOURCE-SESSIONS.json is missing on disk for 46/62 events, so assistant-side neighborhoods cannot be re-read directly.
The lifecycle-thread transcript path is also missing at its canonical location during this rerun; packet-derived user messages remain available, but transcript-side neighborhoods were not used.
<image> markers survive in SOURCE-PACKET.jsonl without image payloads, so visual disputes cannot be re-audited from the seed alone.
